items.title: GPT-5.2 derives a new result in theoretical physics
items.description: A new preprint shows GPT-5.2 proposing a new formula for a gluon amplitude, later formally proved and verified by OpenAI and academic collaborators.

items.title: Introducing Lockdown Mode and Elevated Risk labels in ChatGPT
items.description: Introducing Lockdown Mode and Elevated Risk labels in ChatGPT to help organizations defend against prompt injection and AI-driven data exfiltration.

items.title: Scaling social science research
items.description: GABRIEL is a new open-source toolkit from OpenAI that uses GPT to turn qualitative text and images into quantitative data, helping social scientists analyze research at scale.

items.description: How OpenAI built a real-time access system combining rate limits, usage tracking, and credits to power continuous access to Sora and Codex.

items.title: Amazon halts Blue Jay robotics project after less than 6 months
items.description: Amazon said Blue Jay's core tech will be used for other robotics projects and the employees who worked on it were moved to other projects.

items.title: World Labs lands $1B, with $200M from Autodesk, to bring world models into 3D workflows
items.description: The partnership will see the two companies exploring how World Labs’ models can work alongside Autodesk’s tools, and vice versa, starting with a focus on entertainment use cases.

items.title: Google’s AI music maker is coming to the Gemini app
items.description: Google has given Gemini the ability to spit out AI-generated music, courtesy of DeepMind's latest audio model. items.title: Google adds music-generation capabilities to the Gemini app
items.description: Users will be able to use text, images, and videos as a reference to generate music.

items.title: Kana emerges from stealth with $15M to build flexible AI agents for marketers
items.description: Kana, a new AI marketing startup from the founders of Rapt and Krux, has raised $15 million to build customizable, agent-based marketing tools.

items.title: Microsoft says Office bug exposed customers’ confidential emails to Copilot AI
items.description: Microsoft said the bug meant that its Copilot AI chatbot was reading and summarizing paying customers' confidential emails, bypassing data-protection policies.

items.title: OpenAI pushes into higher education as India seeks to scale AI skills
items.description: OpenAI says its India education partnerships aim to reach more than 100,000 students, faculty, and staff over the next year.

items.title: Perplexity joins anti-ad camp as AI companies battle over trust and revenue
items.description: AI search startup Perplexity is distancing itself from ads amid fears users won't trust chatbots with an agenda to upsell. items.title: Meta’s new deal with Nvidia buys up millions of AI chips
items.description: Meta has struck a multiyear deal to expand its data centers with millions of Nvidia's Grace and Vera CPUs and Blackwell and Rubin GPUs. items.title: Google’s AI search results will make links more obvious
items.description: Google says it will now display links more prominently inside its AI-powered features in Search. items.title: Google announces dates for I/O 2026
items.description: It's official: Google I/O 2026 will take place from May 19th to 20th. items.title: Apple is reportedly planning to launch AI-powered glasses, a pendant, and AirPods
items.description: Apple is pushing ahead with plans to launch its first pair of smart glasses, along with an AI-powered pendant and camera-equipped AirPods, according to a report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. items.title: WordPress’ new AI assistant will let users edit their sites with prompts
items.description: Starting on Tuesday, WordPress users can edit their websites using the new AI assistant built into the platform's site editor and media library, TechCrunch reports. items.title: Laurie Spiegel on the difference between algorithmic music and ‘AI’
items.description: In 1986, electronic music pioneer Laurie Spiegel created Music Mouse, a way for those with a Mac, Atari, or Amiga computer to dabble in algorithmic music creation. items.description: After cramming AI into every inch of its smartphones, appliances, and other devices, Samsung is now increasingly slapping it across social media.
